purpose: Defines the minimum source_manifest.json field contract required for source_intake.py to execute successfully

required_fields.description: Relative path (from REPO_ROOT) to a binary-readable file used as source boundary identity. May be a true archive (.tar, .zip) or a MANIFEST_FILE_PROXY (e.g., a SHA256 manifest file standing in for a local directory source). Must be readable as binary.
required_fields.validation: file must exist; SHA256 must match sha256 field

required_fields.description: Expected SHA256 hexdigest of the file at archive_path. Used to verify boundary integrity.
required_fields.validation: 64-character lowercase hex string; must match computed hash of archive_path

required_fields.description: Relative path (from REPO_ROOT) to the root directory containing the client source files. Used for file inventory and Phase 2 validation.
required_fields.validation: directory must exist; must contain at least 1 file

optional_fields.description: Expected number of files in extracted_path. If present, inventory result validates actual count against this value.
optional_fields.validation: optional; if present, actual rglob file count must match

optional_fields.description: Pre-computed aggregate hash of the entire source directory. Recorded in intake_manifest.json for traceability but not recomputed by source_intake.py.
optional_fields.validation: not validated by source_intake.py — recorded as-is from source_manifest

optional_fields.description: Source version identifier (e.g., git commit hash of the client source). Recorded in intake_manifest.json for traceability.

manifest_file_proxy_pattern.description: When source_type is LOCAL_DIRECTORY, the source is not a true archive. A SHA256 manifest file (e.g., fastapi-backend.manifest.sha256) is used as archive_path to satisfy Phase 1's binary-readable file requirement.
manifest_file_proxy_pattern.how_it_works: The manifest file is hashed (SHA256 of the manifest file itself) and compared to source_manifest['sha256']. This provides stable boundary identity without requiring a true archive.

compatibility_with_run_client_pipeline.phase_01: source_intake.py boundary validation is equivalent to phase_01_source_boundary. Both compute SHA256 of archive_path and compare to source_manifest['sha256']. Outputs are more detailed (boundary result JSON vs. print).
compatibility_with_run_client_pipeline.phase_02: source_intake.py inventory is equivalent to phase_02_intake. Both check extracted_path exists and count files via rglob. Outputs are more detailed (file list JSON vs. print).
compatibility_with_run_client_pipeline.contract_alignment: source_intake.py is designed to be run before run_client_pipeline.py, producing durable intake artifacts that persist in the run directory. It does not replace Phase 1 or Phase 2 — it supplements them.
